context.description: A chapter-like section titled "Die Faust des Riesen" reflects on consciousness as a passenger within the body and contrasts ordinary bodily limits with an extreme survival state. It uses a vivid training-and-attack scenario to describe how the body can suddenly shift into maximum performance, sharpened senses, and urgent escape mode.
normalization_notes: German prose translated into English control language.
No explicit chapter number visible; section heading preserved.

rows.paragraph_text: The body needs fixed conditions to function optimally, while consciousness is framed as the passenger choosing between compliance and survival pressure. A training exertion scene escalates into a sudden attack, triggering a fully activated emergency state with heightened senses, no fatigue, and immediate flight.
rows.text_content: Consciousness, body limits, and survival mode.
